title: Swapping two cups — the tempting wrong way
problem: Cup a holds juice and cup b holds milk. Swap them, so cup a ends with milk and cup b ends with juice.
source:
a = "juice"
b = "milk"
a = b
b = a

trace.note: Cup a (the left cup) now holds juice.
Cup b (the right cup) now holds milk. Now we want to swap them — the obvious idea is to copy one cup into the other.
trace.checkpoint_prompt: Before you step: after `a = b` runs, what will cup a and cup b hold? (Does this one line swap them?)

trace.note: We pour cup b into cup a. But look: cup a's juice is gone, overwritten by milk. Both cups now hold milk — and we never saved the juice anywhere.
We pour cup a into cup b, but cup a is already milk. Both cups end up holding milk; the juice is lost for good. CPython — not the teacher — shows the swap failed.
